title: New York City Launches 'Get Online NYC' Digital Equity Campaign
detail: New York City's Office of Technology and Innovation launched the 'Get Online NYC' campaign in April 2026, featuring an interactive map of hundreds of free computer labs, public Wi-Fi hotspots, and digital literacy resources across the five boroughs. The campaign targets an estimated 400,000 NYC households without home internet access — disproportionately low-income, elderly, and immigrant communities. The initiative integrates NYC's Big Apple Connect free broadband program, library-based digital skills classes, and low-cost device access under a unified public outreach effort, positioning New York as a model for how wealthy cities can systematically address internal digital divides within high-income country contexts.

sources.name: GovTech: What's New in Digital Equity — Get Online NYC Campaign

media.caption: New York City Get Online NYC digital equity campaign launch, April 2026

title: FCC Schedules April 30 Vote on Satellite Spectrum-Sharing Rules That Could Lower Internet Costs
detail: The US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) confirmed it will vote on April 30, 2026, on new spectrum-sharing rules that would allow satellite operators like SpaceX's Starlink and Amazon's Kuiper to negotiate private frequency-sharing agreements with terrestrial wireless providers. The proposed rules would loosen interference restrictions and allow more efficient use of satellite spectrum, potentially improving speeds and reducing service costs for end users. If approved, the framework would represent the most significant update to satellite spectrum regulation in decades and could accelerate competition between LEO satellite providers in rural and underserved markets globally.

sources.name: 5G Store: FCC Rule Makes Starlink Faster and Cheaper in 2026

media.caption: FCC to vote April 30 on satellite spectrum-sharing rules that could benefit Starlink and Kuiper users
